name: Chartway
description_short: Chartway Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative offering banking, lending, and investment services to individuals and businesses with competitive rates and community focus.
description_long:
Chartway Credit Union is a federally-chartered, member-owned credit union operating under NCUA insurance with routing number 251481614. Founded on cooperative principles, it serves both personal and business members through physical branches and digital channels. The institution positions itself as a community-focused alternative to traditional banks, emphasizing member benefits and local engagement through the Chartway Promise Foundation and community giving initiatives.

Chartway provides a comprehensive suite of financial products across three main categories: Spend & Save (high-yield checking up to 5.00% APY, savings accounts, certificates, money market accounts), Borrow (auto loans, credit cards with rewards, boat/RV financing, home equity lines of credit at introductory 3.69% APR, mortgages starting at 5.785% APR, personal loans from 8.24% APR, and credit builder loans), and Plan & Protect (insurance, investment services, IRAs, loan protection). They offer specialized student accounts and business banking solutions including commercial real estate loans, merchant services, and treasury management.

Chartway distinguishes itself through member-centric features including free Greenlight integration for checking accounts, scholarship programs ($4,000 annual directors' memorial scholarships), subscription management tools in online banking, promotional offerings like 3x fuel rewards points and 90-day payment deferrals, and multiple access channels including video banking, virtual assistants, and direct teller ITMs. The organization emphasizes fraud prevention with dedicated alerts and a volunteer program alongside its foundation work. Business members receive tailored services unavailable at typical banks.

As a credit union, Chartway operates on a not-for-profit model returning surplus earnings to members rather than shareholders, potentially offering more favorable rates on deposits and loans. However, membership eligibility requirements may apply, and availability of specific products and rates varies by region. The organization maintains competitive positioning within the credit union space but operates within the constraints of member-owned governance structures.
services: Savings accounts and Smart Steps Savings (up to 4.50% APY)
Share certificates and money market accounts
Auto loans with promotional 90-day payment deferral options
Credit cards with rewards program (3x points on fuel and home improvement)
Home equity lines of credit (HELOC) at 3.69% introductory APR
Credit builder loans for credit establishment
Business checking and business credit cards
Commercial real estate loans and SBA lending
Online and mobile banking with subscription management
Video banking and virtual assistant support
pros: High-yield checking accounts offering up to 5.00% APY, significantly above national bank averages
Introductory HELOC rate of 3.69% APR with up to $500 appraisal fee coverage
Free Greenlight debit card and app integration with Chartway checking for family financial management
Credit builder loans specifically designed to establish or improve credit history
Comprehensive business banking including commercial real estate loans, merchant services, and treasury management
Annual $4,000 directors' memorial scholarships for student members pursuing degrees
Multiple access channels including video banking, virtual assistants, ITMs, and physical branch locations
cons: Membership eligibility requirements apply (not open to all consumers like traditional banks)
Rates vary significantly by region, making advertised rates non-guaranteed for all members
Limited transparency on membership fees, account minimums, or eligibility criteria from website alone
Geographic service limitations with branch/ATM network dependent on membership affiliation
Personal loan APR floor of 8.24% may not be competitive for excellent-credit borrowers
best_for: Member-eligible individuals seeking high-yield savings and checking accounts with community banking values
Homeowners looking to refinance or access home equity with competitive promotional rates
Business owners requiring comprehensive commercial banking and merchant services beyond personal banking
Credit-building borrowers seeking credit builder loans and financial education resources
diagnosis: Chartway Credit Union is best for consumers eligible for membership seeking competitive rates on savings and lending products combined with community-focused banking values and member benefits. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility requirements apply and rates vary significantly by region, so prospective members should verify eligibility and confirm specific product rates before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Chartway — Credit Union in Virginia Beach, VA | CreditDoc
meta_description: Chartway — NCUA-insured credit union in Virginia Beach, VA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
